response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founded GISCollective, built GIS applications for Green Map) and has proven low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second with async I/O and caching. However, his systems-level work was primarily in DLang rather than modern C++ or Rust, his last hands-on C/C++ role ended in 2015, and he has no direct routing or navigation guidance experience. Recent roles skew toward full-stack TypeScript product work, so an interview would need to probe whether his systems skills transfer to a C++-centric routing pipeline.
strengths: Deep geospatial experience: founded and maintains an open-source geo-data platform used in production
Low-latency, high-throughput backend experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers, caching)
Broad stack versatility with strong Linux, Docker, CI/CD, and testing/TDD craftsmanship
concerns: Modern C++ experience is dated (last professional C/C++ role ended in 2015); no Rust listed
No direct routing, map-tiling, or navigation guidance system experience
Recent years focused on web/full-stack TypeScript product work rather than systems programming
